channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Trump called FIFA chief to review Balogun’s red card — Report
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
US President Donald Trump reportedly called FIFA President Gianni Infantino to review and reverse Folarin Balogun’s World Cup red card suspension. Read mor
Read More: https://punchng.com/trump-called-fifa-chief-to-review-baloguns-red-card-report/

item.title: Carlos Queiroz resigns as Ghana coach after World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Carlos Queiroz has resigned as Ghana coach following the Black Stars’ exit from the World Cup. Learn more about his departure and Ghana’s future.
Read More: https://punchng.com/carlos-queiroz-resigns-as-ghana-coach-after-world-cup-exit/

item.title: UPDATED: Norway beat Brazil 2-1 to reach World Cup quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Erling Haaland scored twice as Norway stunned Brazil 2-1 to advance to the World Cup quarter-finals. Read about the dramatic upset and Norway’s historic wi
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-norway-beat-brazil-2-1-to-reach-world-cup-quarter-finals/

item.title: Brazil stunned as Haaland's masterclass sends Norway into last eight
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Erling Haaland dumped Brazil out of the World Cup at the last-16 stage as his brilliant late double secured a 2-1 win for Norway to set up a quarter-final against England or Mexico.

item.title: Gotterup wins John Deere Classic title after thrilling final round
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Chris Gotterup produced a weekend-best 62 with a stunning nine birdies in his final round to claim the John Deere Classic title, finishing 20 under in a thriller in Illinois.

item.title: Defence lawyer challenges court’s jurisdiction over arraignment of alleged Mexican-Nigerian drug cartel
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government, has condemned the killing of two Nigerians in the Republic of South Africa. The post Defence lawyer challenges court’s jurisdiction over arraignment of alleged Mexican-Nigerian drug cartel appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Insurgency: Air Force chief calls for intelligence-driven joint operations
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Chief of the Air Staff (CAS), Air Marshal Sunday Kelvin Aneke, has advocated greater intelligence sharing, inter-agency collaboration and the effective deployment of air power as critical tools for tackling Nigeria’s rapidly evolving security challenges. The post Insurgency: Air Force chief call

item.title: Mutu’s acquittal remains valid despite fresh forfeiture judgment – Onyejiwe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Teddy Onyejiuwe has said emerging facts indicate that the recent judgment of the Federal High Court in Abuja ordering the forfeiture of funds linked to a company associated with Nicholas Mutu has not invalidated or superseded an earlier judgment of a court of coordinate jurisdiction, which discharge

item.title: World Cup: Haaland eliminates Brazil, fires Norway into historic quarter-finals
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Norway will now face the winners of the Round of 16 clash between England and Mexico in the quarter-finals The post World Cup: Haaland eliminates Brazil, fires Norway into historic quarter-finals appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Shelter-in-place order amid storm at Azteca before Mexico-England
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Supporters are told to stay in their seats as an electrical storm approaches Azteca Stadium before Mexico v England in the last 16 of the World Cup.

item.title: Queiroz leaves Ghana following World Cup exit
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Carlos Queiroz steps down as Ghana head coach after three months in charge following their World Cup exit.

item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria

Speaking during the training on Sunday in Kano, Abdulaziz sa

item.title: Jonathan Denies Report Of ₦500bn Offer To Split South-South Votes
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former President Goodluck Jonathan has dismissed as false a media report alleging that he was offered ₦500 billion to contest the 2027 presidential election in order to split votes in the South-South region. Jonathan’s Special Adviser on Media, Dr. Ikechukwu Eze, rejected the claim in a statem

item.title: Haaland sends Norway into quarter-finals as Brazil crash out
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Erling Haaland scores twice for Norway as they beat Brazil 2-1 at the New York New Jersey Stadium to reach the quarter-finals of the World Cup for the first time.

item.title: Need to stay awake for England v Mexico? Try this quiz
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England and Thomas Tuchel will want to match the achievements of the nation's famous side that lifted the World Cup trophy in 1966. Can you name every side they have ever faced in the knockouts?

item.title: FG Condemns Killing Of Two Nigerians In South Africa, Demands Justice
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Federal Government has condemned the killing of Nigerian nationals in South Africa and called for thorough investigations to ensure those responsible are brought to justice. In a statement issued on Sunday by the spokesperson of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Kimiebi Imomotimi Ebienfa, the gov

item.title: How a son rescued his father from the rubble of Venezuela’s earthquakes
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: After the June 24 earthquakes, ex-firefighter Jesus Garcia found himself working to save his dad and younger brothers.

Waidi Shaibu, has rallied army chiefs and defence leaders from across Africa to strengthen military cooperation in tackling terrorism, insurgency and other transnational security threats confronting the continent. item.title: Queiroz Resigns As Ghana Head Coach After World Cup Exit
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Carlos Queiroz announced he was leaving his job as Ghana coach after the African side were eliminated from the World Cup at the last-32 stage. Ghana were beaten 1-0 by Colombia in Kansas City on Friday after only registering eight attempts on goal. The much-travelled Quieroz, who coached Iran at the

item.title: Brazil out of 2026 World Cup after loss to Norway
item.dc:creator: Ripples Nigeria
item.description: Five-time champions Brazil have been knocked out of the 2026 FIFA World Cup after losing 2-1 to Norway in a round of 16 encounter on Sunday night. In an entertaining tie in New Jersey, Norway midfielder Patrick Berg had the ball in the back of the net after just four minutes, only for the celebratio

item.title: Genesis Makes Rival Bid for Vault in $3.9 Billion Deal
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Australian gold miner Genesis Minerals Ltd. has launched a last-minute cash and stock bid for Vault Minerals Ltd. which had already agreed to a deal with Regis Resources Ltd.

item.title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools. Companies like Forge Prep

item.title: OPEC+ raises output levels again despite tumbling crude prices
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Major oil producers on Sunday agreed once again to modestly increase their crude production, although, as in previous months, the hike is largely symbolic until a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran sticks and the Strait of Hormuz is fully reopened to shipping traffic.

item.title: JUST IN: Norway make history, beat Brazil to qualify for quarterfinal
item.dc:creator: SportingLife NG
item.description: Norway have beaten five time World Cup champions Brazil 2-1 in the Round of 16 of the ongoing FIFA World Cup. The Scandinavian side will now made their first ever World Cup quarterfinal, as they face either Mexico or England. The first half ended goalless with either side unable to break through.&#1

item.title: Oil Edges Lower on OPEC Increase, Dollar Steady: Markets Wrap
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Oil opened lower as energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz persisted and OPEC+ signaled higher supplies. Traders were also focused on the Korean won’s first day of 24-hour trading.

item.title: World Cup shocker: Haaland’s brace sends Brazil crashing out
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Deep into stoppage time, Schjelderup again turned provider, slipping a precise pass to Haaland on the edge of the penalty area. The Manchester City striker calmly fired into the bottom-right corner to complete his brace and seal a memorable victory for Norway.

Femi Gbajabiamila, over the controversy surrounding the alleged Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC). The post AYC

item.title: FIFA lifts Folarin Balogun's World Cup suspension after Trump calls Infantino
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump called FIFA president Gianni Infantino and asked him to review the World Cup suspension of US striker Folarin Balogun, a source familiar with the matter said on Sunday. FIFA later lifted Balogun's one-match ban, clearing him to face Belgium in the last 16 after his red card

item.title: 2027: Avoid Atiku-Amaechi ticket, southern political progressive warns ADC
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: From Charity Nwakaudu, Abuja The Southern Political Progressives Amalgamation Forum (SPPAF) has warned the African Democratic Congress (ADC) against fielding what it described as a “legally vulnerable” presidential ticket, cautioning that a proposed alliance between former Vice President

item.title: World Cup 2026: Ronaldo Remains A Threat To Spain –Gavi
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain midfielder Gavi has dismissed recent criticism of Cristiano Ronaldo ahead of their highly anticipated FIFA World Cup 2026 Round of 16 clash against Portugal. Gavi defended the 41-year-old forward, asserting that the heavy scrutiny comes from outside fans, not his teammates. Read Also:World Cup

item.title: WBF Q2 2026: Baale Makes Case for Standardised Corporate Culture in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Professor Lere Baale, CEO, Business School Netherlands International has made a case for Nigerian organisations to intentionally build cultures around five pillars of responsiveness, respect, reliability, responsibility and relationships if they really want to compete globally. A distinguished globa

item.title: 【今朝の5本】仕事を始める前に読んでおきたい厳選ニュース
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: ホルムズ海峡、鴻海精密工業、4.7兆円の大型上場、消費税引き下げ、原油相場一変
